task_yaml.task:
Our team has a meeting next week to decide which LLM agent orchestration
framework to adopt. The candidates are langchain, llama_index, crewAI,
pydantic-ai, and langgraph. Star counts alone don't mean much — I want
to see which of these five was actually maintained during Q1 2026
(2026-01-01 to 2026-03-31), whose community is growing, and who still
gets discussed on Hacker News and Stack Overflow.

Could you pull all those dimensions and give me an evaluation report?
A few lines of numbers per framework, a composite ranking, and a final
recommendation backed by data.

For ranking I'd like something simple: take the Q1 PR count, release
count, issue count, and HN story count as 4 signals. Rank each
dimension, then average the ranks — lowest average wins.

Also, if a framework's core contributor owns dozens of other repos on
GitHub, that signals a broad ecosystem from the team — include that
info too.

Additionally, check how much Python dependency overlap there is among
these 5 frameworks. Pull each repo's pyproject.toml (langchain and
langgraph are monorepos — the actual packages are under
libs/langchain/ and libs/langgraph/ respectively; crewAI is under
lib/crewai/; pydantic-ai is under pydantic_ai_slim/; llama_index
mainly look at llama-index-core/) and extract each framework's runtime
dependencies list. Then do set operations: which packages appear in ≥3
frameworks as "shared infrastructure"? Which appear in all 5? I'm
guessing pydantic and similar are universal — this tells me what the
"ecosystem hubs" of the LLM agent space are, and also reflects how
similar the five teams' design philosophies are.

Repos: langchain-ai/langchain, run-llama/llama_index,
crewAIInc/crewAI, pydantic/pydantic-ai, langchain-ai/langgraph.

Put the report and data files under /tmp_workspace/results/.
